# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kubernetes deployment manifests for microservices. Add annotations to control Istio sidecar injection behavior. Set sidecar.istio.io/inject to manage proxy injection per deployment. This is for our service mesh deployment strategy.
# Category: istio_sidecar_injection_disabled

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
   name: microservice-deployment # Name of deployment. Replace with actual value if needed based on service names in the environment
specs:   
 - replicas: 3                       # Number or type(ReplicaSet, Statefulset) for number/type replica set you want to use (default is Deployment). Default=Deployment  
   selector:                         # Selector labels that match Kubernetes pod names. Replace with actual label if needed based on your service-name and namespace  e.g 'app_label1' or whatever suits best for a deployment of microservices in k8s environment    app:yourAppName (default)
     matchLabels:                     # Match labels to select the pod(S). Replace with actual label if needed based on your service-name and namespace e.g 'app_label1' or whatever suits best for a deployment of microservices in k8s environment    app:yourAppName  (default)
       app: yourService                # Name that matches the pod you want to select, replace with actual name if needed based on service names   yoursername     (mandatory field e.g 'yoursusername')     
         ports:                         # Define port for each sidecar in container spec    Ports should be same as microservice defined by user which is handling traffic from ingress  and other services will consume this traffic, usually you define one open-port per service   (mandatory e.g '80')
           - name: http                   # Name for the port that matches with container spec     Replace it to actual if needed based on your use case    ('http' or whatever suits best in a microservice scenario)  (default="HTTP")     53,1297   (# Port number of pods inside k8s Pod and also exposed by sidecar ingress controller e.g '5604')
               protocol: TCP              # Protocol for the port that matches with container spec     Replace it to actual if needed based on your use case    ('TCP' or whatever suits best in a microservice scenario)  (default="TCP")     1297        (# Port number of pods inside k8s Pod and also exposed by sidecar ingress controller e.g '5604')
           - port: "3000->3000"(Microservice to expose outside the cluster)   # Protocol for ports that should be accessible from outisde this pod  (default="TCP")     Replace it with actual if needed based on your use case e.g '5604' or any suitable values
           - port: "8123->9372"(Ingress to Ingress controller)    # Protocol for ports that should be accessible from ingress  (default="TCP")     Replace it with actual if needed based on your use case e.g '5604' or any suitable values
         resources:                     # Resources request/limits set within the pod   k8s resource requirements and limits for one service instance inside a Pod, replace accordingly    (mandatory)       cpu="1",memory="2G"(default),cpu-corespace("0.5")  e.g '{"cpu":"3","memories": "4GB"}', {"limitsCPU/MEMORY":'<limit>','requestsCPU/MEmory':'<quota>'}
           requests:                    # Resources request for each sidecar in container spec    Requests and limits are set within the pod, usually one single CPU core should be allocated to handle traffics from all services.   (mandatory)      cpu="0",memory=256Mi(default),cpu-corespace("1")  e.g '{"limitsCPU/MEMORY":'4GB','requests":"3"}', {"limits CPU / MEMORy':<limit>,'Requests'/Memory ': <quota>  }
           limits:                     # Resources request and limit for each sidecar in container spec    Limits is set within the pod, one should not over-allocate memory/cpu.   (mandatory)      cpu="0",memory=128Mi(default),memories("4GB")  e.g '{"limitsCPU"/MEMORy':'3', "requests":"6"}',' {"limit CPU / MEMORY":<Limit>,'Request/Memory ': <quota>  }
   template:                          # Specifies the pod that will be created by Kubernetes for this app (mandatory)    Replace it with actual if needed based on your use case  e.g 'name="microservice-deployment"' or any suitable values     ('app' in yaml must match exactly to name field; i.e., replace "yourAppName",you can make sure you have used right names as mentioned above')
       metadata:                       # Specifies the pod information that will be created by Kubernetes   Replace it with actual if needed based on your use case  e.g 'name="microservice-deployment"' or any suitable values     ('app' in yaml must match exactly to name field; i.e., replace "yourAppName",you can make sure you have used right names as mentioned above')
           labels:                      # Specifies the label that will be added for Kubernetes pods   Replace it with actual if needed based on your use case e,g 'app' or whatever suits best in a microservices scenario  ('yoursusername', etc.) (mandatory)    app=yourAppName(default), yoursername
           annotations:                 # Annotation for control Istio sidecar injection behavior   Replace it with actual if needed based on your use case e.g 'sidecar-injector' or whatever suits best in a microservices scenario  ('enabled', etc.) (mandatory)    enabled=true(default),
